info.name: Notification API
info.description:
Complete Notification System API - Multi-channel notifications with templates, scheduling, and preferences

Source: specs/notification/notification.pillar.v2.yml

item.name: Message Lifecycle

request.description:
Queue a notification message for delivery.

Idempotency: Via UNIQUE(message_key)
Send notification scheduled for future delivery
Pick next queued message and prepare for delivery (worker endpoint)
Mark message as successfully delivered
Mark message as failed after exhausting all retry attempts
Reset failed message for manual retry
Cancel a queued or scheduled message

item.description: Message lifecycle management - send, process, track delivery

item.name: Delivery Tracking

request.description:
Record delivery attempt result (success or failure)

Idempotency: Via Idempotency-Key header
Record failed delivery attempt with error details

item.description: Track delivery attempts and provider responses

request.description:
Create a new notification template

Idempotency: Via UNIQUE(code, version, locale, channel)
Create SMS template (no subject)
Update template - creates new version, archives old
Deactivate template - prevents new messages from using it
Activate inactive template

Schedule notification for specific future time

Idempotency: Via Idempotency-Key header
Create multi-step reminder workflow schedule
Execute scheduled notification (worker endpoint)
Cancel pending scheduled notification

item.name: User Preferences

request.description: Get notification preferences (auto-creates if not exists)
Update preferences with quiet hours configuration
Opt out of all notifications
Enable and configure email notifications
Enable and configure SMS notifications
Disable push notifications

item.description: Manage user notification preferences and channel settings

body.raw:
{
  "code": "welcome_email",
  "name": "Welcome Email",
  "channel": "email",
  "locale": "en",
  "subject_tpl": "Welcome to GCPro, {{first_name}}!",
  "body_tpl": "Hi {{first_name}},\n\nWelcome to GCPro! We're excited to have you on board.\n\nGet started: {{dashboard_url}}\n\nBest regards,\nThe GCPro Team",
  "version": "v1"
}

request.description: Phase 5: Processes all pending notification_schedule rows whose fire_at <= NOW(). Called by cron every minute. For grace_expiry schedules, emits GRACE_EXPIRY_DUE which triggers policy freeze.

item.name: Phase 7D — Crowd Charge Notification
item.description: Phase 7D: CROWD_MEMBER_CHARGED → push notification to insured member via template 'crowd_member_charged'

body.raw: {"code": "crowd_member_charged", "channel": "push", "title": "Contribution Charged", "body": "Your contribution of {{paid_amount}} has been deducted for period {{crowd_period_id}}. Remaining: {{remaining_amount}}", "locale": "en", "version": "v1"}

request.description: Create the notification template required by CrowdChargeNotificationHandler. The handler looks up template by code='crowd_member_charged' and gracefully skips if not found.

After CROWD_MEMBER_CHARGED fires, verify a notification_message was created for the member. CrowdChargeNotificationHandler resolves account_id from crowd_member_charge.insurant_id and calls NotificationWorkflowService.sendNotificationMessage.

item.name: Phase 8C — Payment Notifications
item.description: Phase 8C: PAYMENT_SUCCEEDED / PAYMENT_FAILED → push notification to account holder via templates 'payment_succeeded' / 'payment_failed'

body.raw: {"code": "payment_succeeded", "channel": "push", "title": "Payment Successful", "body": "Your payment of {{amount}} {{currency}} for {{purpose_code}} has been processed successfully.", "locale": "en", "version": "v1"}

request.description: Seed the 'payment_succeeded' template. PaymentNotificationHandler looks this up on PAYMENT_SUCCEEDED; skips gracefully if not found.

body.raw: {"code": "payment_failed", "channel": "push", "title": "Payment Failed", "body": "Your payment of {{amount}} {{currency}} could not be processed. Please try again.", "locale": "en", "version": "v1"}

request.description: Seed the 'payment_failed' template. PaymentNotificationHandler looks this up on PAYMENT_FAILED.
After PAYMENT_SUCCEEDED fires, verify a notification_message was queued with message_key=payment_succeeded_intent_{intent_id}. account_id comes directly from the event payload.
After PAYMENT_FAILED fires, verify a push notification was queued via template 'payment_failed'.

item.name: Phase 9C — OTP Delivery (Registration Token)
item.description: Phase 9C: REGISTRATION_TOKEN_ISSUED → OTP/magic-link delivery via template 'otp_delivery'. Server auto-generates 6-digit OTP when no otp_hash provided.

body.raw: {"code": "otp_delivery", "channel": "sms", "title": "Your OTP Code", "body": "Your {{purpose}} OTP is: {{otp}}. Expires at {{expires_at}}. Do not share.", "locale": "en", "version": "v1"}

request.description: Seed the 'otp_delivery' template for SMS channel. RegistrationTokenIssuedHandler looks this up on REGISTRATION_TOKEN_ISSUED. payload_vars: otp, purpose, channel_value, expires_at.

item.name: 9C.2 Issue Registration Token (triggers OTP generation + notification)

request.description: POST without otp_hash → server generates 6-digit OTP, hashes it, emits REGISTRATION_TOKEN_ISSUED with otp_plain in payload. RegistrationTokenIssuedConsumer picks it up and queues OTP notification.
After REGISTRATION_TOKEN_ISSUED fires, verify notification_message was queued with template 'otp_delivery' for the channel_value (phone/email).

exec: pm.test('OTP notification queued', () => pm.expect(Array.isArray(json) ? json.length > 0 : json.id).to.be.ok);

item.name: Phase 9D — Login Alert (User Logged In)
item.description: Phase 9D: USER_LOGGED_IN → login alert notification via template 'login_alert'

body.raw: {"code": "login_alert", "channel": "push", "title": "New Login Detected", "body": "A new login was detected on platform {{platform}}. If this wasn't you, contact support.", "locale": "en", "version": "v1"}

request.description: Seed the 'login_alert' template for push channel. UserLoggedInHandler looks this up on USER_LOGGED_IN.

item.name: 9D.2 Verify: Login Alert Queued After Login

request.description: After USER_LOGGED_IN fires, verify a push notification was queued via template 'login_alert'. Graceful skip if template not seeded.
